task_completion.description: Verify that the agent uses the AstroImageJ GUI (specifically the Data Processor or Image Calculation tools) to perform the calibration steps.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing the AstroImageJ main toolbar, the 'Data Processor' window, or 'Image Computation' dialogs being active and configured.

task_completion.description: Verify that a Master Bias frame has been created and saved to the 'reduced' directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: File manager or AstroImageJ file browser showing a file named 'Master_Bias.fits', 'MBias.fits', or similar within the '~/AstroImages/calibration_project/reduced/' directory.

task_completion.description: Verify that a Master Dark frame has been created and saved to the 'reduced' directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: File manager or AstroImageJ file browser showing a file named 'Master_Dark.fits', 'MDark.fits', or similar within the '~/AstroImages/calibration_project/reduced/' directory.

task_completion.description: Verify that a Master Flat frame has been created and saved to the 'reduced' directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: File manager or AstroImageJ file browser showing a file named 'Master_Flat.fits', 'MFlat.fits', or similar within the '~/AstroImages/calibration_project/reduced/' directory.

task_completion.description: Verify that the science frames have been calibrated and saved to the 'reduced' directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: File manager showing multiple image files in the 'reduced' directory (distinct from the master frames), often with prefixes/suffixes indicating calibration (e.g., 'calibrated_', '_out') or matching the number of input science frames.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent used the AstroImageJ GUI and did not perform the reduction using command-line scripts (e.g., Python/Astropy, IRAF) in a terminal.
integrity.visual_evidence: Absence of terminal windows running data reduction scripts; presence of active AstroImageJ GUI windows.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent manually configured the reduction steps (e.g., selecting directories, setting parameters) rather than simulating activity.
integrity.visual_evidence: Mouse cursor interaction with AstroImageJ menus, file selection dialogs, or the Data Processor configuration panel.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent generated the files locally and did not download pre-reduced files from an external source.
integrity.visual_evidence: No browser activity downloading files; file timestamps in the file manager (if visible) should correspond to the current session time.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task uses real Palomar 200-inch Hale Telescope LFC data. Raw files are located in '~/AstroImages/calibration_project/' with subdirectories 'bias', 'dark', 'flat', and 'science'. The agent must use the AstroImageJ software (a Java-based GUI application). The expected output is a set of master calibration frames (Bias, Dark, Flat) and calibrated science images saved in a 'reduced/' subdirectory. The standard AstroImageJ tool for this is the 'Data Processor' (DP), though manual image math is also possible.
